questions.prompt: What's the minimum observability bar for a production agent?

choices.text: A trace ID propagated through every model and tool call; per-call logs (prompt, response, args, result, latency, error); a persistent store; a UI or query path to inspect traces. Without these, debugging a multi-step agent is re-running and praying.

questions.explanation: Multi-step systems amplify the cost of poor observability. Trace IDs are the spine that holds it all together — every log line carries one, and querying by trace ID assembles the full run. Combine with replay capability for fast debugging.

questions.prompt: A prompt change accidentally disabled prompt caching. Per-call cost doubled overnight. How should this have been caught?

choices.text: A cost dashboard with alerts on per-call cost / daily total / cache hit rate. The drift would have triggered an alert within hours, not weeks. Per-trace cost is a first-class metric on production agent systems — track it alongside latency and error rate.

questions.explanation: Cost is a first-class production metric. p95/p99 cost per trace, daily totals, cache hit rate — all need dashboards with thresholds. Most surprise AI bills are silent regressions caught weeks later because nobody was watching the right number.
